2026-04-20T10:46:46.4511816Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T10:46:46.4512391Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:46.4512837Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:46.4513409Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:46.4513727Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:46.4513969Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:46.4514129Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-20T10:46:46.4514485Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:46.4514732Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:46.4514943Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:46.4515237Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:46.4515512Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-20T10:49:52.1835074Z Filed a documentation issue for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` covering four gaps introduced in the beta.2/beta.3 releases but absent from the README:
2026-04-20T10:49:52.1835689Z 
2026-04-20T10:49:53.2413871Z 1. **Chat isolation** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`) — multi-tenancy enforcement behavior
2026-04-20T10:49:53.2417010Z 2. **`x-agent-session-id` response header** — not documented for clients
2026-04-20T10:49:53.2429878Z 3. **Metadata constraints** — 16 keys / key ≤ 64 / value ≤ 512 limits not mentioned
2026-04-20T10:49:58.3723571Z 4. **Troubleshooting** — missing error cases for conversation validation (404/400) and isolation key mismatch (404)

2026-04-20T10:50:01.9068480Z   - Foundry storage error responses now preserve the full error body
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9068972Z   - Non-400/404/409 error status codes from Foundry storage are normalized to HTTP 500
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9069355Z   
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9069515Z   **1.0.0-beta.2:**
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9069800Z   - Chat isolation key enforcement (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`)
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9070225Z   - `x-agent-session-id` response header on all protocol endpoints
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9070584Z   - Validation for malformed response IDs

2026-04-20T10:50:01.9071410Z   - `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9071659Z   - Startup configuration logging
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9071952Z   - Server version `User-Agent` header on outbound requests
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9072306Z   - Foundry storage URL masking in diagnostic logs
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9072645Z   - Inbound request logging for Tier 1 and Tier 2 setups
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9073129Z

The new headers like `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` and `x-agent-session-id` aren't mentioned, and there's no documentation for the metadata validation constraints or how `previous_response_id` validation now returns errors instead of silently failing. The logging section barely touches on the new `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` and startup configuration logging features that are now available. I'm... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 3099 chars]

2026-04-20T10:50:01.9081085Z ◆ Now I have a clear picture. The package has many samples but let me check if the chat isolation key feature (added in beta.2) is covered in any sample or in the README. The README doesn't mention:
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9081966Z   1. `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header enforcement (added beta.2)
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9082400Z   2. `x-agent-session-id` response header (added beta.2)  
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9083170Z   3. Metadata constraints (16 keys, key ≤ 64, value ≤ 512) (added beta.2)
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9084221Z   4. `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` validation (added beta.3) - important behavior change that now returns 404/400
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9085318Z   
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9085577Z   
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9086195Z   5. Troubleshooting errors for these scenarios
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9086704Z   
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9089358Z   The chat isolation key is a critical multi-tenancy security feature that needs documentation, and the troubleshooting section is missing error cases for isolation key mismatches, invalid response/conversation IDs, and metadata validation failures—all new behaviors in the recent beta releases.
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9091658Z   
2026-04-20T10:50:01.9092007Z   Now I'm filing the issue.

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-20T10:50:02.1436319Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T10:48:10.544393555Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Release History\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n### Other Changes\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.3 (2026-05-05)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` are now validated against the provider before\n  the handler is invoked. Invalid references return HTTP 404/400 immediately instead of being\n  silently ignored. The resolved history item IDs are cached — handlers calling\n  `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse the prefetched result without a second storage lookup.\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Foundry storage error responses now preserve the full error body (`code`, `message`, `param`, `type`)\n  when returned to the client. Previously only the `message` field was forwarded; `param` was lost.\n- Non-400/404/409 error status codes from Foundry storage are no longer proxied as-is; they are\n  normalized to HTTP 500 to avoid leaking upstream infrastructure details.\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.2 (2026-04-17)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Added chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints. When a response is created with\n  `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls must\n  include the same key. Mismatched or missing keys return 404 (indistinguishable from not-found)\n  to ensure tenant isolation.\n- Added `x-agent-session-id` response header on all protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel,\n  Delete, InputItems). The resolved session ID is echoed as a response header per spec §8,\n  with fallback to the `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable for error responses.\n- Added validation for malformed response IDs in both the `response_id` path parameter and the\n  `previous_response_id` request body field. IDs that do not match the expected format (prefix\n  and length) are rejected with 400 and a descriptive error message.\n- Added metadata constraint validation: metadata maps are limited to 16 keys with key length ≤ 64\n  and value length ≤ 512 characters, enforced via the validator pipeline.\n- Added deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation: SHA-256 hash of\n  `\"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\"` truncated to 63 lowercase hex chars.\n  Falls back to random hex when no conversational context is available.\n- Added eager eviction of completed `ResponseExecution` entries from the in-flight tracker,\n  reducing memory pressure for long-running servers.\n- Added `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` — an Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every\n  outbound Foundry storage API call with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation\n  headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\n- Added structured `Information`-level logging to all Responses API endpoints (GET, Cancel, Delete,\n  InputItems) with response ID context. The POST `/responses` creation log now includes response ID,\n  conversation ID, previous response ID, and store flag for full request traceability.\n- Added isolation key presence logging (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey`) to all\n  endpoint handler logs and outbound Foundry storage request logs. Key values are never logged.\n- Added startup configuration logging: storage provider type, default model, fetch history count,\n  and event stream TTL are logged at `Information` level when the host starts.\n- Added server version `User-Agent` header on all outbound Foundry storage API requests. The\n  composed identity from `ServerVersionRegistry` (including developer-registered segments) is\n  prepended to the standard Azure.Core user-agent, read lazily per-request.\n- Added Foundry storage URL masking in diagnostic logs: everything before `/storage` is redacted\n  and query parameters are stripped (except `api-version`) to prevent leaking account and project\n  information.\n- Added inbound request logging for Tier 1 and Tier 2 setups (via `ResponsesServer.Run()` or\n  `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()`). All incoming HTTP requests are logged with method, path, status\n  code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`).\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n- Made `ResponsesActivitySource` internal. The activity source is managed by\n  the framework; handlers do not need to create tracing activities directly.\n- Made `ResponsesTracingConstants` internal. The tracing tag, baggage, and log scope\n  constants are implementation details not needed by handler authors.\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Fixed error response shapes to match the container specification: `invalid_request_error` type\n  for 400 errors and `not_found` message format.\n- Fixed cancel-after-terminal to return `invalid_request_error` type with the correct error shape\n  per the specification.\n- Fixed DELETE endpoint to return 404 (not 400) when the response ID does not exist, aligning\n  with the specification.\n- Fixed cancel-after-delete to return 404 (not-found) per the specification.\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.1 (2026-04-14)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Initial release of the Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses library.\n- ASP.NET Core server library implementing the Azure AI Responses API.\n- `ResponseHandler` abstract class for custom response handling with `CreateAsync` returning `IAsyncEnumerable\u003cResponseStreamEvent\u003e`.\n- `TextResponse` convenience class for simple text-only responses with a single delegate.\n- `ResponseEventStream` builder for full control over SSE event generation with automatic `sequenceNumber`, `outputIndex`, `contentIndex`, and `itemId` tracking.\n- `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators for emitting complete output items in one call:\n  - `OutputItemMessage(string text)` — emits a full text message output item (handles all inner SSE events automatically)\n  - `OutputItemMessage(string text, IEnumerable\u003cAnnotation\u003e annotations)` — emits a text message with file annotations\n  - `OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable\u003cstring\u003e tokens, CancellationToken)` — streams tokens as `response.output_text.delta` events\n  - `OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` — emits a complete function call output item\n  - `OutputItemFunctionCallOutput(callId, output)` — emits a function call output (no deltas)\n  - `OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` — emits a reasoning output item\n  - `OutputItemImageGenCall(resultBase64)` — emits an image generation result with status transitions\n  - `OutputItemStructuredOutputs(output)` — emits an arbitrary structured JSON output item\n  - One-liner convenience generators for all remaining simple output item types: computer calls, local shell calls, function shell calls, apply-patch calls, custom tool call outputs, MCP approval requests/responses, and compaction\n- `ResponseContext` with `GetInputItemsAsync()` (returns `IReadOnlyList\u003cItem\u003e`) and `GetInputTextAsync()` convenience for accessing resolved input items and text content.\n- `IEnumerable\u003cItem\u003e.GetInputText()` extension method for extracting text from any sequence of input items.\n- `CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded()` extension for advanced access to expanded input items as `OutputItem` types.\n- Built-in in-memory response provider and execution tracking.\n- Support for default, streaming, background, and streaming+background response modes.\n- `AgentHostBuilder` convenience methods for zero-config server startup via `ResponsesServer.Run\u003cT\u003e()`.\n- Protocol identity registration with `ServerVersionRegistry` during route mapping.\n- `x-agent-response-id` header validation matching the Responses API specification.\n- Conversation ID round-trip support in both synchronous and SSE streaming modes.\n- OpenTelemetry distributed tracing via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/CHANGELOG.md"},"type":"resource"}]}}}

Add the NuGet package, extend one abstract class (`ResponseHandler`), and the library handles routing, streaming (SSE), background execution, cancellation, caching, and response lifecycle management.\n\n[Source code][source] | [Package (NuGet)][nuget] | [REST API reference][rest_api] | [Product documentation][product_doc]\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\n\nInstall the library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/):\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses --prerelease\n```\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n- An [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/)\n- [.NET 8.0](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) or later\n- An ASP.NET Core application\n\n### Configure the server\n\nThe recommended way to start a Responses server is with the built-in one-line API:\n\n```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConfigureServer_Tier1\nResponsesServer.Run\u003cEchoHandler\u003e();\n```\n\nThis starts a Kestrel server with OpenTelemetry, health checks, server version header, inbound request logging, and your handler mapped to the Responses API endpoints. The `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` package is included as a transitive dependency.\n\nAlternatively, use `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` for more control over service registration and middleware:\n\n```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConfigureServer_Manual\nvar builder = AgentHost.CreateBuilder();\nbuilder.AddResponses\u003cEchoHandler\u003e();\nbuilder.Build().Run();\n```\n\n## Key concepts\n\n### ResponseHandler\n\nThe core abstraction you implement. The library calls `CreateAsync` for each incoming request and delivers the returned `IAsyncEnumerable\u003cResponseStreamEvent\u003e` to clients via SSE.\n\n**`TextResponse` — recommended for text-only responses:**\n\n```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler\npublic class EchoHandler : ResponseHandler\n{\n    public override IAsyncEnumerable\u003cResponseStreamEvent\u003e CreateAsync(\n        Crea

This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully."
2026-04-20T10:50:02.1879917Z       },
2026-04-20T10:50:02.1879982Z       {
2026-04-20T10:50:02.1880071Z         "name": "dispatch_triage",
